strategies.hypothesis: A single independent answer is the minimum-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Direct One

strategies.hypothesis: A small independent plurality is a stable low-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Five-Vote Plurality

strategies.hypothesis: Replicate the Iteration 14 mechanism that paired a broader answer bank with one falsifying reviewer. Against the five-bank one-review proposal, this tests whether additional independent candidates improve recoverable coverage while holding the integration rule and reviewer count fixed.
strategies.name: One-Review Nine-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Blind regeneration raised candidate coverage on two panels but plurality failed to surface the new answers. Three blind fixed-lens regenerators now expand the bank, then one fresh falsifying integrator examines the shuffled unique augmented candidates and may repair them. This isolates candidate integration without changing the generation stage.
strategies.name: Blind Regeneration then Falsifying Integration

strategies.hypothesis: Retain the pooled champion unchanged. Its parallel falsifying repair panel remains the principal replicated control for answer-generating review.
strategies.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Sequential falsification may make later reviewers engage with one concrete proposed solution and critique instead of producing three weakly coordinated parallel repairs. This tests an orthogonal information-flow architecture at the same eight-call nominal cost as the pooled champion.
strategies.name: Sequential Five-Bank Cross-Examination
